body.description: Tumor volume doubling time (VDT) of untreated primary pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) measured from serial multi-detector CT volumetry (no intervening treatment).

inputs.name: vdt_mean
inputs.description: Reported mean tumor volume doubling time (VDT) for untreated PDAC primary tumors measured on serial CT.
inputs.value_snippet: Tumor growth rates were ... corresponding to VDT of 20.0-976.8 days (mean, 132.3 ± 132.1 days).

inputs.name: vdt_sd
inputs.description: Reported standard deviation of tumor volume doubling time (VDT).
inputs.value_snippet: Tumor growth rates were ... corresponding to VDT of 20.0-976.8 days (mean, 132.3 ± 132.1 days).

inputs.name: vdt_min_reported
inputs.description: Reported minimum VDT in the cohort.
inputs.value_snippet: Tumor growth rates were ... corresponding to VDT of 20.0-976.8 days (mean, 132.3 ± 132.1 days).

inputs.name: vdt_max_reported
inputs.description: Reported maximum VDT in the cohort.
inputs.value_snippet: Tumor growth rates were ... corresponding to VDT of 20.0-976.8 days (mean, 132.3 ± 132.1 days).

inputs.name: n_patients
inputs.description: Number of PDAC patients included (≥2 CT scans, no intervening treatment).
inputs.value_snippet: One hundred patients with histologically proven pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma examined with at least two CT scans with no intervening treatment were included.

inputs.name: lower_bound_vdt
inputs.description: Physical lower bound for doubling time used for sampling (VDT cannot be negative).

inputs.name: n_mc_samples
inputs.description: Monte Carlo sample size for parametric bootstrap of the VDT distribution.

inputs.name: rng_seed
inputs.description: Random seed for reproducible Monte Carlo sampling.

calibration_target_estimates.distribution_code:
def derive_distribution(inputs, ureg):
    import numpy as np

    # Reported cohort summary stats (assumed Normal in sampling step)
    mean = inputs['vdt_mean']
    sd = inputs['vdt_sd']

    # Monte Carlo controls
    n_samples = int(inputs['n_mc_samples'].magnitude)
    seed = int(inputs['rng_seed'].magnitude)

    # Physical bound
    lower = inputs['lower_bound_vdt']

    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)

    # Parametric bootstrap from Normal(mean, sd), then enforce VDT >= 0
    samples = rng.normal(mean.magnitude, sd.magnitude, size=n_samples) * mean.units
    samples = np.maximum(samples, lower)

    median_obs = np.median(samples)
    q25 = np.percentile(samples, 25)
    q75 = np.percentile(samples, 75)
    iqr_obs = q75 - q25
    ci95_obs = [np.percentile(samples, 2.5), np.percentile(samples, 97.5)]

    return {'median_obs': median_obs, 'iqr_obs': iqr_obs, 'ci95_obs': ci95_obs}

scenario.description: Natural/untreated primary PDAC growth measured on at least two serial multi-detector CT scans with no intervening treatment; VDT computed from CT-measured tumor volumes.

measurements.computation_code:
def compute_measurement(time, species_dict, ureg):
    """Compute tumor doubling time from instantaneous exponential growth rate.

    Note: Clinical paper estimates VDT from CT tumor volume. In the model, V_T.C1
    is proportional to tumor burden/volume, so doubling time is invariant to the
    proportionality constant.
    """
    import numpy as np

    tumor = species_dict['V_T.C1']  # array-like, tumor cells at multiple timepoints

    # Central difference derivative at middle timepoint
    dt = time[1] - time[0]
    dtumor_dt = (tumor[2] - tumor[0]) / (2 * dt)

    # Exponential growth rate k (1/day)
    k = dtumor_dt / tumor[1]

    ln2 = np.log(2.0) * ureg.dimensionless
    tvdt = ln2 / k

    return tvdt.to('day')

body.study_overview: This retrospective clinical imaging study quantified the natural (untreated) growth kinetics of primary PDAC by calculating tumor volume doubling time from serial CT scans obtained before any treatment.
body.study_design: Histologically proven PDAC patients who had ≥2 multi-detector CT scans with no intervening treatment were included; tumor diameters and volumes were measured on CT and VDTs were computed from changes in CT-derived tumor volume over time.
body.derivation_explanation: The paper reports a cohort mean ± SD and range for CT-based VDT. We represent uncertainty by parametric bootstrap: sample VDT from a Normal(mean, SD) distribution and enforce the physical constraint VDT ≥ 0 days via clipping. Summary statistics (median, IQR, 95% interval) are computed from Monte Carlo samples.

key_assumptions.text: Sampling distribution: VDT values are approximated as Normally distributed using the reported cohort mean and SD, then truncated at 0 days to enforce physical feasibility.

Model mapping: doubling time computed from model tumor burden species V_T.C1 is assumed equivalent to CT volume doubling time because doubling time is invariant under proportional scaling between tumor cells and tumor volume.

Timing anchor: the measurement is mapped to diagnosis-time (offset_days = 0) for calibration convenience, even though the clinical study used two pre-treatment CT timepoints spanning an interval after initial imaging.
Bound handling: negative sampled VDTs (an artifact of the Normal approximation) are clipped at 0 days rather than using an explicit truncated-normal likelihood.

body.key_study_limitations: The abstract does not provide stage-stratified VDTs; therefore the extracted estimate likely reflects a mixed clinical population rather than strictly locally advanced disease. The VDT is derived from serial CT imaging and may be sensitive to segmentation/measurement variability and irregular tumor shape. Our uncertainty reconstruction assumes a parametric Normal form because individual patient-level VDTs are not reported in the abstract.

primary_data_source.title: Time to Progression of Pancreatic Cancer: Evaluation with Multi-Detector Computed Tomography

body.description: Fraction of intratumoral CD4+ T cells that are regulatory T cells (Tregs; CD4+CD25+Foxp3+) measured by flow cytometry from resected primary PDAC tumors (tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes; gating of Tregs within CD4+ T cells).

inputs.name: treg_percent_mean_reported
inputs.description: Reported mean Treg percentage among CD4+ T cells in PDA tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (percent units encoded as 'dimensionless' here; conversion handled via percent_to_fraction).
inputs.value_table_or_section: Results → 'Up-regulation of CD4+T, CD8+T and Treg cells in PDA tissue' (Figure 1D text)
inputs.value_snippet: “We also observed that the proportions of CD4+ T cells (PDA-TIL vs. C-PT: 59.47±0.5974% vs. 12.6±1.343%) and Tregs (PDA-TIL vs. C-PT: 17.8±0.7075% vs. 0.01±0.005%) in TILs were markedly increased compared to C-PTLs (both P<0.001) ( Figure 1B, 1C and 1D ).”

inputs.name: treg_percent_sem_or_sd_reported
inputs.description: Reported uncertainty accompanying the mean percentage (paper reports '17.8±0.7075%'; type of uncertainty not explicitly labeled adjacent to the value).
inputs.value_table_or_section: Results → 'Up-regulation of CD4+T, CD8+T and Treg cells in PDA tissue' (Figure 1D text)

inputs.name: n_fcm_pda_patients
inputs.description: Number of PDAC patients included in the flow cytometry (FCM) analysis.
inputs.value_snippet: “Forty-five patients were analyzed using FCM, and 183 patients were analyzed using only IHC.”

inputs.name: percent_to_fraction
inputs.description: Unit conversion factor from percent (%) to fraction.

inputs.name: assume_reported_uncertainty_is_sem
inputs.description: Assumption flag: treat the reported '±' as SEM (1.0=true). If instead the paper intended SD, set this to 0.0 and treat reported value as SD directly.

inputs.name: lower_bound_fraction
inputs.description: Lower bound for a biological fraction.

inputs.name: upper_bound_fraction
inputs.description: Upper bound for a biological fraction.

inputs.name: n_mc_samples
inputs.description: Number of Monte Carlo samples for parametric bootstrap.

inputs.name: rng_seed
inputs.description: Random seed for Monte Carlo sampling (for reproducibility).

calibration_target_estimates.distribution_code:
def derive_distribution(inputs, ureg):
    import numpy as np

    # Reported summary (percent units carried as 'dimensionless' and converted explicitly)
    mean_pct = inputs['treg_percent_mean_reported']
    err_pct = inputs['treg_percent_sem_or_sd_reported']
    pct_to_frac = inputs['percent_to_fraction']

    # Convert to fraction
    mean = (mean_pct * pct_to_frac).to(ureg.dimensionless)

    # Interpret the reported ± as SEM if requested; otherwise as SD
    assume_sem = inputs['assume_reported_uncertainty_is_sem']
    n = inputs['n_fcm_pda_patients']
    # SD = SEM * sqrt(n) if SEM; else SD = reported err
    sd_if_sem = (err_pct * pct_to_frac * np.sqrt(n.magnitude)) * ureg.dimensionless
    sd_if_sd  = (err_pct * pct_to_frac).to(ureg.dimensionless)

    # Blend using the flag (avoids hard-coded branching constants)
    sd = (assume_sem * sd_if_sem + (1.0 * ureg.dimensionless - assume_sem) * sd_if_sd).to(ureg.dimensionless)

    # Monte Carlo sampling with truncation to [0,1]
    n_samples = int(inputs['n_mc_samples'].magnitude)
    seed = int(inputs['rng_seed'].magnitude)
    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)

    samples = rng.normal(mean.magnitude, sd.magnitude, size=n_samples) * ureg.dimensionless
    lb = inputs['lower_bound_fraction'].to(ureg.dimensionless)
    ub = inputs['upper_bound_fraction'].to(ureg.dimensionless)
    samples = np.clip(samples, lb, ub)

    median_obs = np.median(samples)
    iqr_obs = (np.percentile(samples, 75) - np.percentile(samples, 25))
    ci95_obs = [np.percentile(samples, 2.5), np.percentile(samples, 97.5)]

    return {
        'median_obs': median_obs * ureg.dimensionless,
        'iqr_obs': iqr_obs * ureg.dimensionless,
        'ci95_obs': [ci95_obs[0] * ureg.dimensionless, ci95_obs[1] * ureg.dimensionless]
    }

scenario.description: Untreated (no prior chemo/radiation/other medical interventions) resectable PDAC patients undergo surgical tumor resection; tumor tissue is processed into single-cell suspensions; tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes are analyzed by flow cytometry to quantify Tregs among CD4+ T cells.

measurements.computation_code:
def compute_measurement(time, species_dict, ureg):
    """Fraction of CD4+ T cells that are Tregs in tumor.

    Model mapping assumption:
      - CD4 pool in tumor = Th + Th_exh + Treg
      - Treg pool in tumor = Treg

    Returns: dimensionless fraction.
    """
    treg = species_dict['V_T.Treg']
    th = species_dict['V_T.Th']
    th_exh = species_dict['V_T.Th_exh']

    cd4_total = treg + th + th_exh
    frac = treg / cd4_total
    return frac.to(ureg.dimensionless)

body.study_overview: Study evaluated the abundance of tumor-infiltrating Tregs and other T-cell subsets in human pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, relating intratumoral immune composition to clinicopathologic features and prognosis.
body.study_design: Resected PDAC tumor tissue and control pancreatic tissue were processed for flow cytometry; T-cell subsets were quantified, including CD4+CD25+Foxp3+ Tregs gated within CD4+ T cells; patients had not received chemo/radiation/other medical interventions prior to sampling.
body.derivation_explanation: The paper reports a central estimate for intratumoral Tregs as a percentage within CD4+ T cells (PDA-TIL: 17.8±0.7075%). We convert percent to fraction using an explicit percent_to_fraction input (0.01). Because the paper does not clearly label whether the ± value is SD or SEM at the point of reporting, we include an explicit assumption flag (assume_reported_uncertainty_is_sem). If SEM, we convert to SD using SD = SEM * sqrt(n) with n taken from the paper’s FCM cohort size (45). We then perform a parametric bootstrap by sampling from a normal distribution centered on the mean (fraction) with the derived SD, truncated to [0,1], and compute median, IQR, and 95% interval from Monte Carlo samples.

key_assumptions.text: The reported uncertainty in “17.8±0.7075%” is treated as SEM (not SD), because the magnitude appears small for inter-patient variability; SD is therefore computed as SEM * sqrt(n) with n=45.

Clinical timing is represented as diagnosis-timed baseline (offset_days = 0.0) because the measurement is pre-treatment and obtained at primary tumor resection; exact days from diagnosis to surgery are not provided.
Model CD4 pool is approximated as Th + Th_exh + Treg in the tumor compartment to match the experimental gating of Tregs within CD4+ T cells.
A truncated normal distribution is used for the fraction to enforce biological bounds [0,1].

body.key_study_limitations: Although the paper provides a numeric mean±(error) for the intratumoral Treg percentage, it does not clearly specify at the point of reporting whether the ± term is SD or SEM; this introduces ambiguity in uncertainty calibration. The cohort includes resected PDAC patients, but detailed stratification explicitly limited to strictly resectable cases (vs borderline) and burden characterization are not fully specified for the FCM subset.

primary_data_source.title: An Increased Abundance of Tumor-Infiltrating Regulatory T Cells Is Correlated with the Progression and Prognosis of Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma
